---
title: "Claude Code Prompt — LexMind README.md"
output: html_document
---

# Instructions

Copy everything inside the code block below and paste it into Claude Code.
It will create a professional README.md for the LexMind project.

---

```
Create D:\Projects\LexMind\README.md with this exact content.
This is the main README for the LexMind GitHub repository.
It must be professional, clean, and readable on GitHub.
Use proper Markdown formatting throughout.

COMPLETE FILE CONTENT:

# LexMind

> AI-powered legal research assistant. Upload legal documents, ask questions in plain English, and get grounded answers with article citations — every claim verified before it reaches you.

![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-3.11+-blue)
![FastAPI](https://img.shields.io/badge/FastAPI-0.115-green)
![Next.js](https://img.shields.io/badge/Next.js-16-black)
![LangGraph](https://img.shields.io/badge/LangGraph-0.2-orange)
![Tests](https://img.shields.io/badge/Tests-86%20passing-brightgreen)

---

## What Is LexMind?

LexMind is a multi-agent AI system for legal document research.
You upload a contract, law, or any legal document. You ask a question.
Four specialized AI agents work in sequence to find the most relevant
sections, generate a grounded answer, and verify its quality before
returning it to you.

Every answer:
- Is grounded **strictly** in the uploaded document — no hallucination
- Cites the exact article number for every claim
- Is scored on groundedness, citation accuracy, and relevance
- Can reference previous questions in the same research session

---

## Demo

| Step | What Happens |
|---|---|
| Upload | Drop a PDF contract onto the upload page |
| Ingest | System parses, chunks by article, embeds, stores in ChromaDB |
| Ask | Type a question in the Research page |
| Retrieve | Hybrid dense + BM25 search finds top 5 relevant chunks |
| Reason | LLM generates a cited answer using only those chunks |
| Verify | Critic agent scores the answer — rejects if below threshold |
| Return | Grounded answer with citations and quality scores displayed |

---

## Architecture

### Ingestion Pipeline — 5 Stations

A raw document enters one end. A searchable knowledge base comes out the other.

```
Raw Document (PDF / DOCX / TXT)
        ↓
Station 1 — Parser
        Reads the file. Identifies structure.
        Labels each line as heading (level 1/2/3) or paragraph.
        ↓
Station 2 — Chunker
        Groups elements by article boundary.
        Article 47 and all its sub-clauses stay together in one chunk.
        ↓
Station 3 — Embedder
        Converts each chunk to a 1024-dimensional vector using E5-large.
        passage: prefix for chunks. query: prefix for search queries.
        ↓
Station 4 — Vector Store
        Stores vectors + text + metadata permanently in ChromaDB on disk.
        Embed once. Search forever.
        ↓
Station 5 — Pipeline
        Orchestrates all 4 stations in a single ingest_document() call.
        ↓
Knowledge Base — searchable, persistent, never re-embedded
```

### Query Pipeline — 4 Agents

Four agents run in sequence for every question.

```
User Question
        ↓
Orchestrator Agent
        Classifies the query type:
        factual | analytical | comparison | summarisation
        Selects the right retrieval strategy and prompt template.
        ↓
Retrieval Agent
        Step 1: Dense search via ChromaDB (semantic similarity)
        Step 2: BM25 keyword search (exact term matching)
        Step 3: Merge + deduplicate (up to 40 candidates)
        Step 4: Cross-encoder reranking → top 5 chunks returned
        ↓
Reasoning Agent
        Formats chunks as context.
        Calls LLM with grounding prompt (citations required, no external knowledge).
        Parses citations from the response.
        ↓
Critic Agent
        Scores the answer on three dimensions (0.0–1.0 each):
          Groundedness   — is every claim supported by a chunk?
          Citation Accuracy — are the article numbers correct?
          Relevance      — does the answer address the question?
        If any score is below threshold → reject and regenerate (max 2 times).
        ↓
Final Answer — grounded, cited, quality-verified
```

---

## Tech Stack

| Layer | Technology | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| Embeddings | intfloat/multilingual-e5-large | 1024-dim vectors, 93 languages, runs locally — no API cost |
| Vector DB | ChromaDB | Persistent on-disk vector storage and similarity search |
| Keyword Search | rank-bm25 | BM25 keyword search for exact term matching |
| Reranking | cross-encoder/ms-marco-MiniLM-L-6-v2 | Reranks top-40 candidates to select best 5 |
| Agent Framework | LangGraph | Multi-agent graph with conditional routing and shared state |
| LLM | Google Gemini / OpenAI / Anthropic | Configurable via .env |
| Backend | FastAPI + SQLAlchemy | REST API with Pydantic validation and Swagger docs |
| Database | SQLite | Persistent chat sessions and message history |
| Frontend | Next.js 16 + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS | React application |

---

## Project Structure

```
LexMind/
├── src/
│   ├── ingestion/
│   │   ├── parser.py              # PDF/DOCX/TXT parser with heading detection
│   │   ├── chunker.py             # Legal-aware article-boundary chunker
│   │   ├── embedder.py            # E5-large embedder with lazy loading
│   │   ├── vector_store.py        # ChromaDB wrapper with similarity search
│   │   └── ingestion_pipeline.py  # Orchestrates all 4 stations in one call
│   ├── agents/
│   │   ├── orchestrator.py        # Query type classification
│   │   ├── retrieval_agent.py     # Hybrid dense+BM25 search + cross-encoder
│   │   ├── reasoning_agent.py     # Grounded answer generation
│   │   └── critic_agent.py        # Quality scoring and rejection loop
│   ├── graph/
│   │   ├── state.py               # LexMindState TypedDict (shared agent state)
│   │   └── graph.py               # LangGraph wiring and run_query()
│   ├── api/
│   │   ├── main.py                # FastAPI app, CORS, startup events
│   │   ├── schemas.py             # Pydantic request/response models
│   │   └── routes/                # ingest, query, documents, sessions routers
│   └── db/
│       ├── database.py            # SQLAlchemy engine and session factory
│       └── models.py              # Session and Message ORM models
├── tests/
│   ├── ingestion/                 # 44 tests
│   └── agents/                    # 42 tests
├── frontend/                      # Next.js application
├── data/                          # ChromaDB, BM25 index, SQLite sessions (gitignored)
├── docs/                          # Reference and planning documents
├── .env.example                   # Environment variable template
├── pyproject.toml                 # Python package configuration
└── requirements.txt
```

---

## Getting Started

### Prerequisites

- Python 3.11+
- Node.js 18+
- A virtual environment set up at `venv/`

### 1. Clone and configure

```bash
git clone https://github.com/yourusername/LexMind.git
cd LexMind
cp .env.example .env
```

Edit `.env` and add your LLM credentials:

```env
LLM_PROVIDER=google
GOOGLE_API_KEY=your_key_here
```

### 2. Install Python dependencies

```bash
python -m venv venv
.\venv\Scripts\activate        # Windows
pip install -r requirements.txt
pip install -e .
```

### 3. Install frontend dependencies

```bash
cd frontend
npm install
cd ..
```

### 4. Start the backend

```bash
uvicorn src.api.main:app --reload --port 8000
```

- API: http://localhost:8000
- Swagger UI: http://localhost:8000/docs
- Health check: http://localhost:8000/health

### 5. Start the frontend

```bash
cd frontend
npm run dev
```

Open http://localhost:3000

> **Important:** Always stop uvicorn with Ctrl+C before closing the terminal.
> Closing the window without Ctrl+C leaves orphan processes holding port 8000.

---

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| LLM_PROVIDER | google | LLM provider: google, openai, anthropic, azure |
| GOOGLE_API_KEY | — | Google Gemini API key |
| OPENAI_API_KEY | — | OpenAI API key (if using OpenAI) |
| ANTHROPIC_API_KEY | — | Anthropic API key (if using Anthropic) |
| EMBEDDING_MODEL | intfloat/multilingual-e5-large | HuggingFace embedding model |
| EMBEDDING_DEVICE | cpu | cpu, cuda, or mps |
| CHUNK_SIZE | 1500 | Max characters per chunk before splitting |
| TOP_K_FINAL | 5 | Final chunks returned after reranking |
| GROUNDEDNESS_THRESHOLD | 0.75 | Minimum groundedness score to pass |
| CITATION_THRESHOLD | 0.85 | Minimum citation accuracy score to pass |
| RELEVANCE_THRESHOLD | 0.70 | Minimum relevance score to pass |
| MAX_REGENERATIONS | 2 | Max times Critic rejects before accepting |
| CHROMA_DB_PATH | ./data/chroma_db | ChromaDB storage directory |
| BM25_INDEX_PATH | ./data/bm25_index.pkl | BM25 index file |
| SESSIONS_DB_PATH | ./data/sessions.db | SQLite sessions database |

---

## API Reference

| Method | Endpoint | Description |
|---|---|---|
| POST | /api/ingest | Upload PDF/DOCX/TXT — runs full ingestion pipeline |
| POST | /api/query | One-shot query — runs agent pipeline, returns answer + scores |
| GET | /api/documents | List all ingested documents with chunk counts |
| DELETE | /api/documents/{doc_id} | Remove document and all its chunks from ChromaDB |
| POST | /api/sessions | Create a new research session |
| GET | /api/sessions | List all sessions sorted by most recently updated |
| GET | /api/sessions/{id} | Get session with full message history |
| POST | /api/sessions/{id}/message | Send message — agent pipeline with conversation context |
| DELETE | /api/sessions/{id} | Delete session and all its messages |
| GET | /health | API status and knowledge base statistics |

Full interactive documentation at http://localhost:8000/docs

---

## Tests

86 tests total. All tests run in under 2 seconds — they test deterministic
logic with no LLM calls, no network requests, and no file I/O.

```bash
# Run all tests
python -m pytest tests/ -v

# Run by category
python -m pytest tests/ingestion/ -v   # 44 tests
python -m pytest tests/agents/ -v      # 42 tests
```

| File | Tests | Covers |
|---|---|---|
| tests/ingestion/test_parser.py | 7 | Parsing, heading detection, element classification |
| tests/ingestion/test_chunker.py | 7 | Article-boundary chunking, overlap, doc_id generation |
| tests/ingestion/test_embedder.py | 8 | E5 embedding, query/passage prefixes, semantic similarity |
| tests/ingestion/test_vector_store.py | 10 | ChromaDB storage, search, metadata filtering, delete |
| tests/ingestion/test_pipeline.py | 8 | End-to-end ingestion, duplicate handling, batch ingestion |
| tests/agents/test_state.py | 5 | LexMindState creation and field defaults |
| tests/agents/test_orchestrator.py | 8 | Query classification, JSON parsing, fallback handling |
| tests/agents/test_reasoning_agent.py | 12 | Citation extraction, chunk formatting, prompt templates |
| tests/agents/test_critic_agent.py | 10 | Score parsing, feedback composition, threshold checks |
| tests/agents/test_graph.py | 7 | Routing logic, graph compilation, conditional edges |

---

## Key Design Decisions

**Grounded answers only**
The LLM is explicitly forbidden from using any knowledge outside the
retrieved chunks. Every claim must be cited. If the document does not
contain enough information the answer says so — it never halluminates.

**Hybrid search**
Dense vector search (E5-large) finds semantically similar chunks even
when words differ. BM25 finds exact article references and specific terms.
A cross-encoder then reranks the merged top-40 to select the best 5.

**Critic loop**
After the Reasoning Agent generates an answer the Critic scores it on
three dimensions. If any score falls below its threshold the answer is
rejected and regenerated with specific feedback — up to MAX_REGENERATIONS
times (default 2). After that the best available answer is returned.

**Session memory without polluting retrieval**
Conversation history is passed to the Reasoning Agent for coherent
follow-up answers. But retrieval always uses the raw question only.
This prevents previous questions from biasing the vector search toward
earlier topics.

**Lazy loading**
The E5 model (1.3 GB) loads only on the first ingestion call.
ChromaDB connects only on the first request. Startup is instant.

---

## License

MIT

---

*Built with LangGraph, FastAPI, ChromaDB, and Next.js*

After creating the file show me the first 20 lines to confirm it was saved correctly.
```
